What's going on everyone? How to sell prospects before you even say a word to them? And so what I'm going to walk through here is the physical presentation of how you're going to present the facility, present yourself, etc. that can pre-sell a prospect way more than anything else. And this is all the little details and nuances that we've put together and that I put together in my gyms that worked really well for us, that worked really well for a lot of our clients. And I just want to give you something that will help you sell whether you work with us or not. Like this is an awesome, awesome thing that will help you out. Okay, so as soon as a person walks in the door, okay, one, you have to greet them within 10 seconds. The worst thing in the world is when someone just has to sit there waiting to be helped. So have people run over. There has to be something that's in your facility that's inbred any new person. You want to greet them immediately. And when they see that kind of effort, someone walk, you know, run right over, people appreciate that kind of thing. So that has to be something that's a, like a core tenet of the culture that you have within your, you know, gym with the trainers, front desk, etc. that everyone needs to be greeted within 10 seconds. Okay, as soon as you greet them, you're like, awesome, welcome to, you know, one, two, three fitness, who you're here to meet with. Okay, now if they don't have someone to meet with, and you're like, awesome, let me go grab, let me see if you know, Tom's free, give me two seconds, I'll be right back. And then that person needs to hustle to go find you. Okay, now if they do know they're meeting with Brad or whatever, like awesome, I think Brad's with someone on, but give me two seconds and I'll go get them for you. Right. Do me one favor. This is the second piece. Go and sign in on the sign in sheet that way we can, you know, we just keep track of everything with all of our appointments. The sign in sheet should always be stacked full of appointments all day long. So you can use the same shine and sheet every time, if you want to. But they should see that there's just like all these appointments that are that have been met with before they came in the door. So just when they look at that sign and sheet, they're like, wow, so subconsciously, they're like, there's a lot of, you know, there's already some scarcity that's there. It's like, wow, this guy's like, these people are pretty busy, like people seem to be coming in. That's, that's good. Right. So starts positioning you as a busy facility as a place that, you know, has a lot of clients, right. Then you just need to show them where to sit or stand, preferably said, if you have a waiting room or waiting area. And this be like, awesome, he'll be right out over to me with you. Okay, and everyone needs to have this step because it's good to have someone wait for you even briefly, because it positions you. Why do you think doctors have waiting rooms, right? Why do lawyers have waiting rooms? It's positioning. Okay. So as soon as they've been greeted and you, and you told them, you know, sign in and then sit down, you hand them a flipbook of testimonials. Okay, so it's a big, thick book, like if you remember the baseball collecting cards, if you ever collected cards, the big laminates, you don't want the little nine slots, you want the big ones, but like, it should be one of those big binders. And inside of it, you should have all the five star reviews from all of your sources. So if you don't already have that stuff, a lot of people are like, well, I don't have that many testimonials. Yeah, you do. Every single five star review you have on your Facebook, your Google, your Yelp screenshot those print them out and then put them in that binder. Okay, then also have before and after pictures that they can flip through, try and put these all together. So don't like have all five star reviews and then all pictures and then like, interweave them, right? So five terms and if you can match the five star review to the person, that's like, you know, level 201. But have the reviews have before and after pictures and then something that's working really well right now is having Facebook screenshots and Instagram screenshots of successes. And that could be like, Hey, I lost two inches. Hey, you know, I'm wearing my dress again. Hey, I'm excited for I could finally remember bikini to Cancun, whatever, like we all have those things. And if you don't know where to find them, just click stats or progress or progress pick or transformation inside of your Facebook group, which hopefully you should have a Facebook group for your community. And Facebook will pull them right up and you'll be able to find them real easy. And then you want to just have some pics of the events with the community because you want to show that you're you're a living breathing community of people who are active and you want to show that you're engaged and that your customers love you and all these happy pictures make people feel like, Okay, wow, there's other people who trust this business. You know, and then again, it increases the amount of trust they're going to have with you. And this is before you've even said anything to them. Okay. So the waiting room decor and the sales room decor should be more or less basically like that flipbook coming to life. Okay, it should be really overwhelming and very impressive. Okay. And so you want to have a waiting room, this is one that I actually visited personally. So I like, I went overboard and told him how I want to do it. But basically, even that little white space on the, the blue space on the top, right, like you want it to be floor to ceiling testimonials. And what he's when he printed out there, screenshots or reviews, some, you know, some testimonials on Facebook before and after pictures, all those things you just want to cover your wall. So when someone sits down, they're like, Dang, these people are like really loved, right? Here's some other examples. But even here, like, I wouldn't want that space above the couch, like I want it to be totally covered. And same thing here before and above and below, I want it to be totally covered like plaster, like wallpaper before and afters. Okay, you should get the point. Now, once they walk into your office or your sales area, okay, so we went from waiting room to now we're talking like where they're actually going to get sold. Okay, some people have rooms that people have just an area worth a table, whatever, right? They should see all these testimonials, but they should also see as many accreditations as possible. Okay, so if you don't have a lot of certifications or whatever, just drop 300 bucks on a few online certs that you can frame in your office. So it appears as though you have, you know, four or more certifications minimum, right? We all know in the business that like those things don't matter, because it's all about others that like you, that's like giving getting a driver's license and saying someone's a pro, but in the world of general public, they don't know that certifications don't mean very much. So it's good to have a lot of them on the wall because it gives you more authority and credibility. So it should look something like this, all right, just as many as humanly possible, put all of your trainer's certifications plus yours, just like it should be this huge wall of just like, wow, we're really good, right? Now, that's what they should see when they walk in the sales room testimonials, plus all the accreditations, which should be like behind behind your desk, not behind you, because you're going to not be behind the desk, which is my next point. Okay, so say this is your sales area, all right? You always want to sell ideally, like, hey, you always want to be on the same side of the table. So you can notice here, these front two chairs are more or less on the same side of table, you never want to sit across from someone, okay, because then it just already shows its adversarial, it looks like you're coming against, looks like you're, you know, you're going to go head to head, that's not the point you want to go like kitty corner side to side shoulder to shoulder, or like slightly turned. And this is actually almost an ideal sort of like scenario where you have like, they're semi turned, but semi not. So it's like, it's a great middle ground where people aren't like, if you're going to like truly shoulder to shoulder be weird, because you're not talking like you have to face them, right? So you want to be like, in between against versus like next to so like right about here. Okay. Now, what you're doing that it just makes them feel more comfortable and it makes you feel more casual. Okay. These are just little details that can really add up in a sales process. Okay. And next thing you see this arrow, it says stack of signed contracts is that you want to have a nice stack of like freshly signed contracts in front of you, like this. Sorry for the shutter stock, I was just googling image of stack contracts, but you want to be have a big stack of contracts in front of you are on that table, it doesn't have to be like nine like there, but just you want to have the one stack that's for that specific promotion that someone's coming in for. And if you're like, well, I don't have a stack of contracts, so what you can do is you can take two things you can either take, like all of your EFT contracts, put them there and then put the new ones on top and just put a blank in between so you can keep them separate, or just print out a stack of contracts that are unsigned and put, you know, four or five signed ones on top, and then have two or three unsigned ones next to it. So they see like signed, unsigned, and if there's only two or three unsigned left, then it shows scarcity, right? That's the whole point is that we're trying to show proof and scarcity throughout this whole process, subconsciously, like without them, you don't have to say it, it just the place will exude scarcity exude social proof, so that you don't have to sell it as hard because there are like, if someone's like, well, I'm not sure if this will work, you can be like, really? You know, like, how do I know that this is like that you're like, you're really as good as you say you are, you're like, well, that's what all the creditations are. And here's all the social proof. So it just does the selling for you. So this is one of the key stuff. This is before we even get into the sales conversation that you can do to stack the chips in your favor. All right, so use this stuff, it will help you. And as a call to action at the end, you can always say, hey, do you want one of the last spots and you can point to the contracts to show that you don't have that many spots left. It's an easy call to action in a sales process that works really, really well. So hopefully this is useful for you. Hope you enjoyed this as much as I enjoyed making it for you. And go close, close, close. All right, talk to you soon. All right.
